details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>ORGANIZATION<|>TechGlobal is a stock now listed on the Global Exchange which powers 85% of premium smartphones)
##
("entity"<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>ORGANIZATION<|>Vision Holdings is a firm that previously owned TechGlobal)
##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 3:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,GEO,PERSON
Text:
Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.
######################
Output:

*개인정보 처리 업무의 위탁*

------------------------------



Google은 계열사

<https://policies.google.com/privacy/key-terms?hl=ko&gl=KR#toc-terms-affiliates>

혹은 신뢰하는 업체들에게 Google의 지침과 개인정보취급방침 및 여하의 기밀과  

보안유지 요건들에 맞춰 개인정보를 처리하도록 개인정보를

제공하기도 합니다.



Google은 한국 거주 이용자들에게 Google의 서비스를 보다 원활하고 신속하게  

제공하기 위하여 업체들과 위탁업무계약을 체결하고

있습니다. 자세한 사항은 https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko

<https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko&gl=KR> 에서 확인하실  

수 있습니다.



*개인정보 보호 설정*

------------------------------



사용자는 Google에서 수집하는 정보와 이용 방식에 대해 선택권이 있습니다.  

자세한 사항은 여기에서 확인하세요:

https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#infochoices.



또한 개인정보 보호 진단

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacycheckup?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>을  

방문하여 중요한

개인정보 보호 설정을 검토하고 조정할 수 있습니다.



*정보를 검토하고 업데이트하는 방법*



*내 활동*



내 활동에서 내가 한 검색이나 Google Play 방문 등 Google 서비스를 이용하면서  

생성된 데이터를 검토하고 관리할 수

있습니다. 날짜 및 주제별로 찾아보고 활동의 일부 또는 전부를 삭제할 수  

있습니다.



내 활동으로 이동  

<https://myactivity.google.com/myactivity?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*Google 대시보드*



Google 대시보드에서 특정 제품과 연결된 정보를 관리할 수 있습니다.



대시보드로 이동  

<https://myaccount.google.com/dashboard?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*사용자 개인정보*



이름, 이메일, 전화번호 등 사용자 연락처 정보를 관리합니다.



개인정보로 이동

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacy?utm_source=pp&hl=ko#personalinfo>.

------------------------------



*구글페이먼트코리아 유한회사가 제공하는 구글 페이먼트 서비스를 이용하는  

경우, 아래 구글 페이먼트 개인정보처리방침에서 해당 서비스상의

개인정보 처리내역을 확인하실 수 있습니다.*



*구글 페이먼트 개인정보처리방침:*

https://payments.google.com/payments/apis-secure/get_legal_document?ldo=0&ldt=privacynotice&ldl=ko



찾고 계신 구매, 신용 및/또는 거래 관련 정보는 모든 사용자가 본인의 데이터에  

액세스할 수 있도록 Google에서 제공하는 여러

안전한 온라인 도구를 통해 이미 제공되고 있을 수 있습니다. Google 계정  

<https://myaccount.google.com/>에

로그인하여 Google 서비스 사용 방식에 관한 전반적인 정보를 확인하고 데이터에  

액세스하실 수 있습니다.



Google 테이크아웃을 사용해 거래 내역을 내보내실 수도 있습니다.



    1. takeout.google.com으로 이동합니다.

    2. Google 테이크아웃 페이지가 열리면 *모두 선택 해제*를 클릭합니다.

    3. *Google Pay*를 선택합니다.

    4. 하단에서 *다음 단계*를 클릭합니다.

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

Example 2:

TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.

##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

방식은 사용자가 서비스를 어떻게 이용하고 개인정보 보호 설정을 어떻게  

관리하는지에 따라 다릅니다.

    - *사용자가 생성하거나 제공하는 정보.* 사용자는 Google 계정을 만들 때  

이름과 비밀번호를 포함한 개인정보

     

<https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#footnote-personal-info>를

    Google에 제공합니다. 또한 계정에 전화번호

     

<https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#footnote-phone-number>나  

결제

    정보

    <https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#footnote-payment-info>를

    추가하도록 선택할 수 있습니다. Google 계정에 로그인하지 않더라도, 서비스  

업데이트를 받기 위해 이메일 주소를 제공하는 것과 같이

    정보를 제공하도록 선택할 수 있습니다. 또한 사용자가 서비스를 이용하면서  

생성, 업로드하거나 다른 사람에게 받는 콘텐츠를 수집합니다.

    여기에는 사용자가 작성하거나 수신하는 이메일, 저장하는 사진과 동영상,

액세스할 때 사용하는 앱, 브라우저, 기기

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

제공하기도 합니다.



Google은 한국 거주 이용자들에게 Google의 서비스를 보다 원활하고 신속하게  

제공하기 위하여 업체들과 위탁업무계약을 체결하고

있습니다. 자세한 사항은 https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko

<https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko&gl=KR> 에서 확인하실  

수 있습니다.



*개인정보 보호 설정*

------------------------------



사용자는 Google에서 수집하는 정보와 이용 방식에 대해 선택권이 있습니다.  

자세한 사항은 여기에서 확인하세요:

https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#infochoices.



또한 개인정보 보호 진단

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacycheckup?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>을  

방문하여 중요한

개인정보 보호 설정을 검토하고 조정할 수 있습니다.



*정보를 검토하고 업데이트하는 방법*



*내 활동*



내 활동에서 내가 한 검색이나 Google Play 방문 등 Google 서비스를 이용하면서  

생성된 데이터를 검토하고 관리할 수

있습니다. 날짜 및 주제별로 찾아보고 활동의 일부 또는 전부를 삭제할 수  

있습니다.



내 활동으로 이동  

<https://myactivity.google.com/myactivity?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*Google 대시보드*



Google 대시보드에서 특정 제품과 연결된 정보를 관리할 수 있습니다.



대시보드로 이동  

<https://myaccount.google.com/dashboard?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*사용자 개인정보*



이름, 이메일, 전화번호 등 사용자 연락처 정보를 관리합니다.



개인정보로 이동

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacy?utm_source=pp&hl=ko#personalinfo>.

------------------------------



*구글페이먼트코리아 유한회사가 제공하는 구글 페이먼트 서비스를 이용하는

*구글 페이먼트 개인정보처리방침:*

https://payments.google.com/payments/apis-secure/get_legal_document?ldo=0&ldt=privacynotice&ldl=ko



찾고 계신 구매, 신용 및/또는 거래 관련 정보는 모든 사용자가 본인의 데이터에

안전한 온라인 도구를 통해 이미 제공되고 있을 수 있습니다. Google 계정  

<https://myaccount.google.com/>에

로그인하여 Google 서비스 사용 방식에 관한 전반적인 정보를 확인하고 데이터에  

액세스하실 수 있습니다.



Google 테이크아웃을 사용해 거래 내역을 내보내실 수도 있습니다.



    1. takeout.google.com으로 이동합니다.

    2. Google 테이크아웃 페이지가 열리면 *모두 선택 해제*를 클릭합니다.

    6. *내보내기 생성*을 클릭합니다.

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

Text: . 또한 사용자를 안전하게  

보호한다는 것은 Google 제품 전반에서 개인 파일 및 관련

데이터 저장 기간을 최소화하는 강력한 개인 정보 보호 관행을 적용함을  

의미합니다. Google은 사용자가 더 이상 Google 서비스를

사용하지 않더라도 사용자의 개인 정보를 보호하고 계정에 대한 무단 액세스를  

방지하고자 합니다.



따라서 모든 Google 제품 및 서비스에서 Google 계정의 휴면 기간

<https://support.google.com/accounts?p=inactive_account_policy_email>이  

2년으로

업데이트됩니다. 이 변경사항은 오늘부터 적용되기 시작하며 2년 동안  

로그인하지 않았거나 사용하지 않은 휴면 상태의 모든 Google

계정에 적용됩니다. 휴면 계정 및 계정 내 모든 콘텐츠는 2023년 12월 1일부터  

삭제 대상이 됩니다.



*이번 결정이 미치는 영향:*



    - Google 계정에서 2년 동안 활동을 하지 않았거나 2년 이상 계정을 사용하여  

Google 서비스에 로그인하지 않은 경우가

    아니라면 이번 변경사항으로 아무런 영향을 받지 않습니다.

    - 이 변경사항은 오늘 적용되지만 계정 삭제는 2023년 12월부터 시행됩니다.

    - Google에서 계정을 휴면 상태로 간주하고 조치를 취하거나 계정 콘텐츠를  

삭제하기 전에 사용자 이메일 및 복구 이메일(있는

    경우)로 몇 개의 알림 이메일을 전송합니다. 이러한 알림 이메일은 계정에  

조치를 취하기 최소 8개월 전에 전송됩니다.

    - Google 계정이 삭제되면, 삭제된 해당 Google 계정의 Gmail 주소를 다시  

사용하여 새 Google 계정을 만들 수

    없습니다.



*계정을 활성 상태로 유지하는 방법*



Google 계정을 활성 상태로 유지하는 가장 간단한 방법은 적어도 2년에 한 번  

계정에 로그인하는 것입니다. 지난 2년 동안

Google 계정에 로그인한 적이 있는 경우 계정을 활성 상태로 간주하며 삭제되지  

않습니다.



다음 방법으로도 계정을 활성 상태로 유지할 수 있습니다.



    - 이메일 읽기 또는 보내기

    - Google Drive 사용하기

    - YouTube 동영상 시청하기

    - 사진 공유하기

    - 앱 다운로드하기

    - Google 검색 사용하기

    - 'Google 계정으로 로그인'을 사용하여 서드 파티 앱 또는 서비스에  

로그인하기



이 정책에는 일부 예외사항이 있습니다. 그 예로는 YouTube 채널, 동영상 또는  

댓글이 있는 Google 계정, 잔여 금액이 있는

기프트 카드를 보유한 계정 또는 게시된 애플리케이션이 있는 계정(예: Google  

Play 스토어에서 앱을 호스팅하는 계정)이

있습니다. 이 정책에 대한 다른 예외사항은 여기

<https://support.google.com/accounts?p=inactive_account_policy_email>에서  

확인할

수 있습니다.



또한 Google에서는 Google 계정 관리에 도움이 되는 도구와 Google 테이크아웃

<https://takeout.google.com/>을 사용하여 데이터를 다운로드하는 등 데이터  

백업 기능을 제공하며, 휴면계정 관리자

<https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/3036546>를 통해 특정 기간 동안  

활동이 없는 경우

데이터를 어떻게 할지 계획을 세울 수 있도록 지원합니다.



Google에서는 원한다면 가능한 한 쉽게 계정을 활성 상태로 유지하는 데  

우선순위를 두고 있으며, 이번 변경사항에 따라 계정이

-Goal-

-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity

3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>ORGANIZATION<|>TechGlobal is a stock now listed on the Global Exchange which powers 85% of premium smartphones)
##
("entity"<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>ORGANIZATION<|>Vision Holdings is a firm that previously owned TechGlobal)
##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 3:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,GEO,PERSON
Text:
Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>FIRUZABAD<|>GEO<|>Firuzabad held Aurelians as hostages)
##
("entity"<|>AURELIA<|>GEO<|>Country seeking to release hostages)
##
("entity"<|>QUINTARA<|>GEO<|>Country that negotiated a swap of money in exchange for hostages)
##
##
("entity"<|>TIRUZIA<|>GEO<|>Capital of Firuzabad where the Aurelians were being held)
##
("entity"<|>KROHAARA<|>GEO<|>Capital city in Quintara)
##
("entity"<|>CASHION<|>GEO<|>Capital city in Aurelia)
##
("entity"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>PERSON<|>Aurelian who spent time in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison)
##
("entity"<|>ALHAMIA PRISON<|>GEO<|>Prison in Tiruzia)
##
("entity"<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>PERSON<|>Aurelian journalist who was held hostage)
##
("entity"<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>PERSON<|>Bratinas national and environmentalist who was held hostage)
##
("relationship"<|>FIRUZABAD<|>AURELIA<|>Firuzabad negotiated a hostage exchange with Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>QUINTARA<|>AURELIA<|>Quintara brokered the hostage exchange between Firuzabad and Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>QUINTARA<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Quintara brokered the hostage exchange between Firuzabad and Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>ALHAMIA PRISON<|>Samuel Namara was a prisoner at Alhamia prison<|>8)
##
("relationship"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>Samuel Namara and Meggie Tazbah were exchanged in the same hostage release<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>Samuel Namara and Durke Bataglani were exchanged in the same hostage release<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>Meggie Tazbah and Durke Bataglani were exchanged in the same hostage release<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Samuel Namara was a hostage in Firuzabad<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Meggie Tazbah was a hostage in Firuzabad<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Durke Bataglani was a hostage in Firuzabad<|>2)
<|COMPLETE|>

*개인정보 처리 업무의 위탁*

------------------------------



Google은 계열사

<https://policies.google.com/privacy/key-terms?hl=ko&gl=KR#toc-terms-affiliates>

혹은 신뢰하는 업체들에게 Google의 지침과 개인정보취급방침 및 여하의 기밀과  

보안유지 요건들에 맞춰 개인정보를 처리하도록 개인정보를

제공하기도 합니다.



Google은 한국 거주 이용자들에게 Google의 서비스를 보다 원활하고 신속하게  

제공하기 위하여 업체들과 위탁업무계약을 체결하고

있습니다. 자세한 사항은 https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko

<https://policies.google.com/privacy/additional?hl=ko&gl=KR> 에서 확인하실  

수 있습니다.



*개인정보 보호 설정*

------------------------------



사용자는 Google에서 수집하는 정보와 이용 방식에 대해 선택권이 있습니다.  

자세한 사항은 여기에서 확인하세요:

https://policies.google.com/privacy?hl=ko&gl=KR#infochoices.



또한 개인정보 보호 진단

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacycheckup?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>을  

방문하여 중요한

개인정보 보호 설정을 검토하고 조정할 수 있습니다.



*정보를 검토하고 업데이트하는 방법*



*내 활동*



내 활동에서 내가 한 검색이나 Google Play 방문 등 Google 서비스를 이용하면서  

생성된 데이터를 검토하고 관리할 수

있습니다. 날짜 및 주제별로 찾아보고 활동의 일부 또는 전부를 삭제할 수  

있습니다.



내 활동으로 이동  

<https://myactivity.google.com/myactivity?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*Google 대시보드*



Google 대시보드에서 특정 제품과 연결된 정보를 관리할 수 있습니다.



대시보드로 이동  

<https://myaccount.google.com/dashboard?utm_source=pp&hl=ko>.



*사용자 개인정보*



이름, 이메일, 전화번호 등 사용자 연락처 정보를 관리합니다.



개인정보로 이동

<https://myaccount.google.com/privacy?utm_source=pp&hl=ko#personalinfo>.

------------------------------



*구글페이먼트코리아 유한회사가 제공하는 구글 페이먼트 서비스를 이용하는  

경우, 아래 구글 페이먼트 개인정보처리방침에서 해당 서비스상의

개인정보 처리내역을 확인하실 수 있습니다.*



*구글 페이먼트 개인정보처리방침:*

https://payments.google.com/payments/apis-secure/get_legal_document?ldo=0&ldt=privacynotice&ldl=ko



찾고 계신 구매, 신용 및/또는 거래 관련 정보는 모든 사용자가 본인의 데이터에  

액세스할 수 있도록 Google에서 제공하는 여러

안전한 온라인 도구를 통해 이미 제공되고 있을 수 있습니다. Google 계정  

<https://myaccount.google.com/>에

로그인하여 Google 서비스 사용 방식에 관한 전반적인 정보를 확인하고 데이터에  

액세스하실 수 있습니다.



Google 테이크아웃을 사용해 거래 내역을 내보내실 수도 있습니다.



    1. takeout.google.com으로 이동합니다.

    3. *Google Pay*를 선택합니다.

    4. 하단에서 *다음 단계*를 클릭합니다.

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################

##

##

TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.

Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################

######################

Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.

Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################

Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON

YouTube 서비스를 사용하면서 알아두어야 할 사항을 더 명확하고 쉽게 이해하실  

수 있도록 2022년 1월 5일에 서비스 약관('약관')을 업데이트할 예정입니다.  

이번 변경사항은 YouTube 사용 방식에 영향을 주지 않습니다. 변경사항 요약과  

업데이트된 약관은 여기  

<https://notifications.google.com/g/p/AD-FnExcYuGbTohWgevaCOtogyn-NY8RGEhNojOfEaUtyonulNLQ6CjNjy9eOi0PHZXy2_K7aywOOBz8T1NEaLu3ojZPymftNE8H7qjks4kO84515rIV2a0qTqgnXEquMz4J3w>에서  

확인하실 수 있습니다.

업데이트를 요약하면 다음과 같습니다.

* 알아두어야 할 사항에 대한 명확한 설명 제공: YouTube가 서비스를 개발하고,  

개선하고, 업데이트하는 방식을 더욱 투명하게 공개합니다. 여기에는 변경 및  

업데이트를 하는 이유와 YouTube에서 사용자에게 제공하는 사전 통지에 대한  

자세한 설명이 포함됩니다.

* 가독성 개선을 위한 일반 업데이트: YouTube 약관은 여전히 법률 문서이지만

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.

Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

######################

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.

##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)

-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>

details.prompt: MANY entities and relationships were missed in the last extraction. Remember to ONLY emit entities that match any of the previously extracted types. Add them below using the same format:

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:

##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################
Output:

##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,GEO,PERSON
Text:
Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.
######################

history.content:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################

##

<|COMPLETE|>

Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>ORGANIZATION<|>TechGlobal is a stock now listed on the Global Exchange which powers 85% of premium smartphones)
##
("entity"<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>ORGANIZATION<|>Vision Holdings is a firm that previously owned TechGlobal)
##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 3:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,GEO,PERSON
Text:
Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.
######################

details.prompt:
-Goal-
Given a text document that is potentially relevant to this activity and a list of entity types, identify all entities of those types from the text and all relationships among the identified entities.
 
-Steps-
1. Identify all entities. For each identified entity, extract the following information:
- entity_name: Name of the entity, capitalized
- entity_type: One of the following types: [organization,person,geo,event]
- entity_description: Comprehensive description of the entity's attributes and activities
Format each entity as ("entity"<|><entity_name><|><entity_type><|><entity_description>)
 
2. From the entities identified in step 1, identify all pairs of (source_entity, target_entity) that are *clearly related* to each other.
For each pair of related entities, extract the following information:
- source_entity: name of the source entity, as identified in step 1
- target_entity: name of the target entity, as identified in step 1
- relationship_description: explanation as to why you think the source entity and the target entity are related to each other
- relationship_strength: a numeric score indicating strength of the relationship between the source entity and target entity
 Format each relationship as ("relationship"<|><source_entity><|><target_entity><|><relationship_description><|><relationship_strength>)
 
3. Return output in English as a single list of all the entities and relationships identified in steps 1 and 2. Use **##** as the list delimiter.
 
4. When finished, output <|COMPLETE|>
 
######################
-Examples-
######################
Example 1:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,PERSON
Text:
The Verdantis's Central Institution is scheduled to meet on Monday and Thursday, with the institution planning to release its latest policy decision on Thursday at 1:30 p.m. PDT, followed by a press conference where Central Institution Chair Martin Smith will take questions. Investors expect the Market Strategy Committee to hold its benchmark interest rate steady in a range of 3.5%-3.75%.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution is the Federal Reserve of Verdantis, which is setting interest rates on Monday and Thursday)
##
("entity"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>PERSON<|>Martin Smith is the chair of the Central Institution)
##
("entity"<|>MARKET STRATEGY COMMITTEE<|>ORGANIZATION<|>The Central Institution committee makes key decisions about interest rates and the growth of Verdantis's money supply)
##
("relationship"<|>MARTIN SMITH<|>CENTRAL INSTITUTION<|>Martin Smith is the Chair of the Central Institution and will answer questions at a press conference<|>9)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 2:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION
Text:
TechGlobal's (TG) stock skyrocketed in its opening day on the Global Exchange Thursday. But IPO experts warn that the semiconductor corporation's debut on the public markets isn't indicative of how other newly listed companies may perform.

TechGlobal, a formerly public company, was taken private by Vision Holdings in 2014. The well-established chip designer says it powers 85% of premium smartphones.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>ORGANIZATION<|>TechGlobal is a stock now listed on the Global Exchange which powers 85% of premium smartphones)
##
("entity"<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>ORGANIZATION<|>Vision Holdings is a firm that previously owned TechGlobal)
##
("relationship"<|>TECHGLOBAL<|>VISION HOLDINGS<|>Vision Holdings formerly owned TechGlobal from 2014 until present<|>5)
<|COMPLETE|>

######################
Example 3:
Entity_types: ORGANIZATION,GEO,PERSON
Text:
Five Aurelians jailed for 8 years in Firuzabad and widely regarded as hostages are on their way home to Aurelia.

The swap orchestrated by Quintara was finalized when $8bn of Firuzi funds were transferred to financial institutions in Krohaara, the capital of Quintara.

The exchange initiated in Firuzabad's capital, Tiruzia, led to the four men and one woman, who are also Firuzi nationals, boarding a chartered flight to Krohaara.

They were welcomed by senior Aurelian officials and are now on their way to Aurelia's capital, Cashion.

The Aurelians include 39-year-old businessman Samuel Namara, who has been held in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison, as well as journalist Durke Bataglani, 59, and environmentalist Meggie Tazbah, 53, who also holds Bratinas nationality.
######################
Output:
("entity"<|>FIRUZABAD<|>GEO<|>Firuzabad held Aurelians as hostages)
##
("entity"<|>AURELIA<|>GEO<|>Country seeking to release hostages)
##
("entity"<|>QUINTARA<|>GEO<|>Country that negotiated a swap of money in exchange for hostages)
##
##
("entity"<|>TIRUZIA<|>GEO<|>Capital of Firuzabad where the Aurelians were being held)
##
("entity"<|>KROHAARA<|>GEO<|>Capital city in Quintara)
##
("entity"<|>CASHION<|>GEO<|>Capital city in Aurelia)
##
("entity"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>PERSON<|>Aurelian who spent time in Tiruzia's Alhamia Prison)
##
("entity"<|>ALHAMIA PRISON<|>GEO<|>Prison in Tiruzia)
##
("entity"<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>PERSON<|>Aurelian journalist who was held hostage)
##
("entity"<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>PERSON<|>Bratinas national and environmentalist who was held hostage)
##
("relationship"<|>FIRUZABAD<|>AURELIA<|>Firuzabad negotiated a hostage exchange with Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>QUINTARA<|>AURELIA<|>Quintara brokered the hostage exchange between Firuzabad and Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>QUINTARA<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Quintara brokered the hostage exchange between Firuzabad and Aurelia<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>SAMUEL NAMARA<|>ALHAMIA PRISON<|>Samuel Namara was a prisoner at Alhamia prison<|>8)

("relationship"<|>MEGGIE TAZBAH<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Meggie Tazbah was a hostage in Firuzabad<|>2)
##
("relationship"<|>DURKE BATAGLANI<|>FIRUZABAD<|>Durke Bataglani was a hostage in Firuzabad<|>2)
<|COMPLETE|>
